title: Selection
description: The selection CDK provides directives for creating interactive selection functionality in lists and collections. It supports both single and multiple selection modes, with built-in state management and two-way data binding. The selection system consists of two main directives: axSelectionGroup for the container and axSelectionItem for individual selectable items.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A basic example of using the selection directives.

sections.title: Multiple
sections.description: Enable multiple selection using the multiple property.

props.name: multiple
props.description: Enables multiple selection mode. When true, multiple items can be selected simultaneously.

props.name: disable
props.description: Disables all selection functionality within the group.

props.name: selectedKeys
props.description: Two-way binding for the currently selected keys. Returns a single key for single selection mode or an array of keys for multiple selection mode.

methods.content:
/**
 * Sets the active item(s) by their index position in the selection group.
 *
 * @param restParams - Variable number of index parameters to activate
 * @returns void - No return value. Updates the active state of items at the specified indices.
 */
activeItemByIndex(...restParams: number[]): void

props.name: key
props.description: Required unique identifier for the selection item. Used to track selection state and emit selection events.

props.name: disable
props.description: Disables selection for this specific item.

props.name: isActive
props.description: Signal indicating whether this item is currently selected. Can be used in templates for conditional styling.

events.name: onClick
events.description: Emitted when the selection item is clicked. Provides a reference to the clicked item directive.

methods.content:
/**
 * Toggles the selection state of the item.
 *
 * @returns void - No return value. Updates the isActive signal state.
 */
toggle(): void

/**
 * Selects the item, setting its active state to true.
 *
 * @returns void - No return value. Sets the isActive signal to true.
 */
select(): void

/**
 * Unselects the item, setting its active state to false.
 *
 * @returns void - No return value. Sets the isActive signal to false.
 */
unselect(): void
